column2: Linux registered-user account records are stored in {{c1::/etc/passwd}}.
column3: The records include account attributes such as user and group IDs, home directories, and shells.
column4: Keep general account records separate from the password hashes and aging data stored in /etc/shadow.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 19
column9: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Cloze Linux Accounts Configuration Source::Messer-v140

column2: Linux password hashes and password-aging information are stored in {{c1::/etc/shadow}}.
column3: The file is distinct from the broader user-account records in /etc/passwd.
column4: Do not infer from the name /etc/passwd that it stores the password hashes; the approved notes associate those hashes with /etc/shadow.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 19
column9: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Cloze HighYield Linux Accounts Configuration Source::Messer-v140

column2: Local Linux hostname-to-address mappings are stored in {{c1::/etc/hosts}}.
column3: A local mapping can supply a name result separately from DNS.
column4: Do not confuse local mappings with DNS resolver settings in /etc/resolv.conf.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 19
column9: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Cloze Linux DNS Configuration Source::Messer-v140

column2: Linux filesystem mount definitions are listed in {{c1::/etc/fstab}}.
column3: The mount process reads this configuration to make defined filesystems available through the directory tree.
column4: Think “filesystem table”: the filename fstab points to stored filesystem mount definitions.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 19
column9: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Cloze HighYield Linux Filesystems Configuration Source::Messer-v140

column2: Linux DNS resolver configuration is stored in {{c1::/etc/resolv.conf}}.
column3: Incorrect resolver settings can disrupt services that depend on DNS.
column4: /etc/hosts supplies local name mappings; /etc/resolv.conf controls resolver details.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 19
column9: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Cloze Linux DNS Configuration Source::Messer-v140
